info.title: A tool for managing terminal sessions on the Open Relay daemon.

commands.name: api-key
commands.description: Manage API keys on this (primary) daemon

commands.name: add
commands.description: Generate a new API key and print it once. Keys are independent of node names

arguments.name: NAME
arguments.description: Friendly label for this key (must be unique)

commands.name: help
commands.description: Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

commands.name: ls
commands.description: List all registered API keys (names only; raw values are never stored)

commands.name: remove
commands.description: Remove an API key by name. Nodes using it will be disconnected

arguments.name: NAME
arguments.description: Label of the key to remove

commands.name: attach
commands.description: Attach to a running session

options.name: --node
options.description: Target a secondary node by name

commands.name: daemon
commands.description: Start or stop the daemon process

commands.name: help
commands.description: Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

commands.name: start
commands.description: Launch the daemon. Runs in the foreground unless `--detach` is given

options.name: --detach
options.description: Run the daemon in the background, detached from this terminal

options.name: --no-auth
options.description: Disable HTTP authentication. You will be asked to confirm the security risk

options.name: --no-http
options.description: Disable the HTTP API and web frontend entirely

commands.name: stop
commands.description: Gracefully shut down the running daemon

commands.name: help
commands.description: Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

commands.name: input
commands.description: Send text or keys to a session

options.name: --key
options.description: Send key/control sequence (repeatable): named key or hex byte ('0x1b' or '\x1b' for ESC). Example: -k ctrl -k c -k '0x1b' -k '\x1b'

options.name: --node
options.description: Target a secondary node by name

arguments.name: ID
arguments.description: Session ID to send input to

commands.name: join
commands.description: Manage this daemon's outbound connections to a primary daemon

commands.name: help
commands.description: Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

commands.name: ls
commands.description: List all active join configs on this (secondary) daemon

commands.name: start
commands.description: Connect this daemon to a primary as a named secondary node. Config is persisted across restarts

options.name: --name
options.description: Name this daemon will be known as on the primary (must be unique per primary)

options.name: --key
options.description: API key printed by `oly api-key add` on the primary

arguments.name: URL
arguments.description: HTTP base URL of the primary daemon, e.g. http://primary-host:15443

commands.name: stop
commands.description: Disconnect from a primary and delete the saved join config

options.name: --name
options.description: Name of the join config to stop and remove

commands.name: logs
commands.description: Show session logs

options.name: --tail
options.description: Number of recent lines to display [default: 40]

options.name: --keep-color
options.description: Keep ANSI color codes in output

options.name: --node
options.description: Target a secondary node by name

options.name: --wait-for-prompt
options.description: Block until the session needs input (or exits), then print logs

arguments.name: ID
arguments.description: Session ID to show logs for

commands.name: ls
commands.description: List sessions. Order is most recently created last

options.name: --search
options.description: Filter by title or ID substring (case-insensitive)

options.name: --status
options.description:
Only show sessions with these statuses (repeatable)
Possible values:
- created:  Created but not yet started
- running:  Process is running
- stopping: Shutting down
- stopped:  Exited cleanly
- failed:   Exited with an error
- unknown:  Status could not be determined

options.name: --limit
options.description:
Maximum number of sessions to return
[default: 10]

options.name: --node
options.description: Target a secondary node by name

commands.name: node
commands.description: List secondary nodes currently connected to this (primary) daemon

commands.name: help
commands.description: Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

commands.name: ls
commands.description: List all secondary nodes currently connected to this (primary) daemon

commands.name: start
commands.description: Create a session and run a command. Example: `oly start --detach --title "my fun demo" copilot`

options.name: --detach
options.description: Start the session detached (in the background)

options.name: --node
options.description: Target a secondary node by name

commands.name: stop
commands.description: Stop a session by ID

options.name: --grace
options.description: Seconds to wait for clean exit before forcibly killing [default: 5]

options.name: --node
options.description: Target a secondary node by name
